crypto.c(3) Library Functions Manual crypto.c(3)

NAME

crypto.c - 🔐 Client cryptography: handshake integration, X25519 key exchange, and per-session encryption

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void client_crypto_set_mode (uint8_t mode)
Set crypto mode for handshake (encryption + authentication). int client_crypto_init (void)
Initialize client crypto handshake. int client_crypto_handshake (socket_t socket)
Perform crypto handshake with server. bool crypto_client_is_ready (void)
Check if crypto handshake is ready. const crypto_context_t * crypto_client_get_context (void)
Get crypto context for encryption/decryption. int crypto_client_encrypt_packet (const uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_len, uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_size, size_t *ciphertext_len)
Encrypt a packet for transmission. int crypto_client_decrypt_packet (const uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_len, uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_size, size_t *plaintext_len)
Decrypt a received packet. void crypto_client_cleanup (void)
Cleanup crypto client resources. bool crypto_client_should_rekey (void)
Check if session rekeying should be triggered. int crypto_client_initiate_rekey (void)
Initiate session rekeying (client-initiated). int crypto_client_process_rekey_request (const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)
Process received REKEY_REQUEST packet from server. int crypto_client_send_rekey_response (void)
Send REKEY_RESPONSE packet to server. int crypto_client_process_rekey_response (const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)
Process received REKEY_RESPONSE packet from server. int crypto_client_send_rekey_complete (void)
Send REKEY_COMPLETE packet to server and commit to new key.

Detailed Description

🔐 Client cryptography: handshake integration, X25519 key exchange, and per-session encryption

CORE RESPONSIBILITIES:

1.
Initialize client crypto context with authentication credentials
2.
Perform cryptographic handshake with server during connection
3.
Manage global crypto context for client connection
4.
Provide encryption/decryption functions for secure packet transmission
5.
Support multiple authentication modes (password, SSH key, passwordless)
6.
Handle session rekeying for long-lived connections

CRYPTOGRAPHIC HANDSHAKE ARCHITECTURE:

The handshake follows a multi-phase protocol:

PHASE 0: PROTOCOL NEGOTIATION:

  • Step 0a: Send client protocol version
  • Step 0b: Receive server protocol version
  • Step 0c: Send client crypto capabilities
  • Step 0d: Receive server crypto parameters

PHASE 1: KEY EXCHANGE:

  • Step 1: Receive server's ephemeral public key and send our public key
  • Client generates ephemeral key pair for this session
  • Both sides derive shared secret using X25519 key exchange

PHASE 2: AUTHENTICATION:

  • Step 2: Receive auth challenge and send response
  • Client signs challenge with identity key (if client has identity key)
  • Server verifies client identity (if whitelist enabled)
  • Step 3: Receive handshake complete message

CRYPTO INITIALIZATION:

The client supports three initialization modes:

1.
SSH KEY MODE (--key specified):
•
Parses Ed25519 private key from file or gpg:keyid format
•
Extracts public key for authentication
•
Supports password-protected keys (SSH agent or prompt)
•
Optional password for dual authentication (key + password)
2.
PASSWORD MODE (--password specified):
•
Uses Argon2id key derivation from shared password
•
Both client and server derive same key from password
•
No identity keys required
3.
PASSWORDLESS MODE (no credentials):
•
Generates random ephemeral keys
•
No long-term identity (no authentication)
•
Suitable for trusted networks or testing

GLOBAL CRYPTO CONTEXT:

The client uses a single global crypto context (g_crypto_ctx):

  • Shared across all connection attempts (reused on reconnection)
  • Initialized once per program execution
  • Cleaned up on program shutdown
  • Stores server connection info for known_hosts verification

SERVER IDENTITY VERIFICATION:

Client verifies server identity using known_hosts:

  • Checks server's identity key against ~/.ascii-chat/known_hosts
  • First connection: Prompts user to accept server key
  • Subsequent connections: Verifies key matches stored value
  • Key mismatch: Warns user about potential MITM attack
  • Optional --server-key for explicit server key verification

CLIENT AUTHENTICATION REQUIREMENTS:

When server requires client authentication (whitelist enabled):

  • Client must provide identity key with --key option
  • Client's public key must be in server's --client-keys list
  • Authentication failure results in connection rejection
  • Interactive prompt warns user if no identity key provided

SESSION REKEYING:

Long-lived connections support periodic rekeying:

  • crypto_client_should_rekey(): Checks if rekey is needed
  • crypto_client_initiate_rekey(): Client-initiated rekeying
  • crypto_client_process_rekey_request(): Handles server-initiated rekey
  • Rekeying refreshes encryption keys without reconnecting

ENCRYPTION/DECRYPTION OPERATIONS:

After handshake completion:

  • crypto_client_encrypt_packet(): Encrypts packets before transmission
  • crypto_client_decrypt_packet(): Decrypts received packets
  • Both functions use global crypto context
  • Automatic passthrough when encryption disabled (--no-encrypt)

ALGORITHM SUPPORT:

The client currently supports:

  • Key Exchange: X25519 (Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman)
  • Cipher: XSalsa20-Poly1305 (Authenticated Encryption)
  • Authentication: Ed25519 (when client has identity key)
  • Key Derivation: Argon2id (for password-based authentication)
  • HMAC: HMAC-SHA256 (for additional integrity protection)

ERROR HANDLING:

Handshake errors are handled appropriately:

  • Server disconnection during handshake: Log and return error
  • Protocol mismatch: Log detailed error and abort connection
  • Authentication failure: Log and return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED
  • Network errors: Detect and handle gracefully (reconnection)
  • Invalid packets: Validate size and format before processing

THREAD SAFETY:

Crypto operations are thread-safe:

  • Global crypto context protected by initialization flag
  • Single crypto context per client process (no concurrent handshakes)
  • Encryption/decryption operations are safe for concurrent use
  • Rekeying operations coordinate with connection thread

INTEGRATION WITH OTHER MODULES:

  • main.c: Calls client_crypto_init() during client startup
  • server.c: Calls client_crypto_handshake() during connection
  • protocol.c: Uses encryption functions for secure packet transmission
  • crypto/handshake.h: Core handshake protocol implementation
  • crypto/keys/keys.h: Key parsing and management functions
  • crypto/known_hosts.h: Server identity verification

WHY THIS MODULAR DESIGN:

Separating cryptographic operations from connection management provides:

  • Clear cryptographic interface for application code
  • Easier handshake protocol evolution
  • Better error isolation and debugging
  • Improved security auditing capabilities
  • Independent testing of crypto functionality

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

Version

2.0

See also

server.c For connection establishment and crypto handshake timing

crypto/handshake.h For handshake protocol implementation

crypto/keys/keys.h For key parsing and management

crypto/known_hosts.h For server identity verification

Definition in file src/client/crypto.c.

Function Documentation

int client_crypto_handshake (socket_t socket)

Perform crypto handshake with server. Perform crypto handshake with server

Parameters

socket Connected socket to server

Returns

0 on success, -1 on failure

Definition at line 439 of file src/client/crypto.c.

439                                              {
440   // If client has --no-encrypt, skip handshake entirely
441   if (GET_OPTION(no_encrypt)) {
442     log_debug("Client has --no-encrypt, skipping crypto handshake");
443     return 0;
444   }
445 
446   // If we reach here, crypto must be initialized for encryption
447   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_crypto_lc)) {
448     log_error("Crypto not initialized but server requires encryption");
449     log_error("Server requires encrypted connection but client has no encryption configured");
450     log_error("Use --key to specify a client key or --password for password authentication");
451     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED; // No retry - configuration error
452   }
453 
454   log_debug("Starting crypto handshake with server...");
455 
456   START_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
457 
458   // Step 0a: Send protocol version to server
459   protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
460   client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
461   client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
462   client_version.supports_encryption = g_crypto_mode;    // Send crypto mode bitmask
463   client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;             // No compression for now
464   client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
465   client_version.feature_flags = 0;
466 
467   int result = send_protocol_version_packet(socket, &client_version);
468   if (result != 0) {
469     log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
470     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
471     return -1;
472   }
473   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Protocol version sent successfully");
474 
475   // Step 0b: Receive server's protocol version
476   packet_type_t packet_type;
477   void *payload = NULL;
478   size_t payload_len = 0;
479 
480   result = receive_packet(socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
481   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
482     log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type %u)", packet_type);
483     log_error("Packet type 0x%x (decimal %u) - Expected 0x%x (decimal %d)", packet_type, packet_type,
484               PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION);
485     log_error("This suggests a protocol mismatch or packet corruption");
486     log_error("Raw packet type bytes: %02x %02x %02x %02x", (packet_type >> 0) & 0xFF, (packet_type >> 8) & 0xFF,
487               (packet_type >> 16) & 0xFF, (packet_type >> 24) & 0xFF);
488     if (payload) {
489       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
490     }
491     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
492     return -1;
493   }
494 
495   if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
496     log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu, expected %zu", payload_len,
497               sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
498     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
499     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
500     return -1;
501   }
502 
503   protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
504   memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
505   buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
506 
507   // Convert from network byte order
508   uint16_t server_proto_version = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version);
509   uint16_t server_proto_revision = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision);
510   uint8_t server_mode = server_version.supports_encryption;
511 
512   log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (crypto mode: 0x%02x)", server_proto_version, server_proto_revision,
513            server_mode);
514 
515   // Verify server echoed our mode
516   if (server_mode != g_crypto_mode) {
517     log_error("Server mode mismatch: got 0x%02x, expected 0x%02x", server_mode, g_crypto_mode);
518     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
519     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
520   }
521 
522   // Step 0c: Send crypto capabilities to server
523   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Sending crypto capabilities");
524   crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
525   client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(KEX_ALGO_X25519);
526   client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE);
527   client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305 | CIPHER_ALGO_NONE);
528   client_caps.requires_verification = 0; // Client doesn't require server verification (uses known_hosts)
529   client_caps.preferred_kex = KEX_ALGO_X25519;
530   client_caps.preferred_auth = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_AUTH(g_crypto_mode) ? AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 : AUTH_ALGO_NONE;
531   client_caps.preferred_cipher =
532       ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode) ? CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305 : CIPHER_ALGO_NONE;
533 
534   result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(socket, &client_caps);
535   if (result != 0) {
536     log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities to server");
537     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
538     return -1;
539   }
540   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Crypto capabilities sent successfully");
541 
542   // Step 0d: Receive server's crypto parameters
543   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Receiving server crypto parameters");
544   payload = NULL;
545   payload_len = 0;
546 
547   result = receive_packet(socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
548   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS) {
549     log_error("Failed to receive server crypto parameters (got type %u)", packet_type);
550     if (payload) {
551       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
552     }
553     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
554     return -1;
555   }
556 
557   if (payload_len != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
558     log_error("Invalid crypto parameters packet size: %zu, expected %zu", payload_len,
559               sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
560     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
561     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
562     return -1;
563   }
564 
565   crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params;
566   memcpy(&server_params, payload, sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
567   buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
568 
569   // Convert from network byte order
570   uint16_t kex_pubkey_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.kex_public_key_size);
571   uint16_t auth_pubkey_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.auth_public_key_size);
572   uint16_t signature_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.signature_size);
573   uint16_t shared_secret_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.shared_secret_size);
574 
575   log_debug("Server crypto parameters: KEX=%u, Auth=%u, Cipher=%u (key_size=%u, auth_size=%u, sig_size=%u, "
576             "secret_size=%u, verification=%u)",
577             server_params.selected_kex, server_params.selected_auth, server_params.selected_cipher, kex_pubkey_size,
578             auth_pubkey_size, signature_size, shared_secret_size, server_params.verification_enabled);
579   log_debug("Raw server_params.kex_public_key_size = %u (network byte order)", server_params.kex_public_key_size);
580 
581   // Set the crypto parameters in the handshake context
582   result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&g_crypto_ctx, &server_params);
583   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
584     FATAL(result, "Failed to set crypto parameters");
585   }
586 
587   // Store verification flag - server will verify client identity (whitelist check)
588   // This is independent of whether server provides its own identity
589   if (server_params.verification_enabled) {
590     g_crypto_ctx.server_uses_client_auth = true;
591     g_crypto_ctx.require_client_auth = true;
592     log_info("Server will verify client identity (whitelist enabled)");
593   }
594 
595   // Validate that server chose algorithms we support
596   if (server_params.selected_kex != KEX_ALGO_X25519) {
597     log_error("Server selected unsupported KEX algorithm: %u", server_params.selected_kex);
598     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
599     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
600   }
601 
602   // Validate cipher selection based on negotiated mode
603   bool expect_cipher = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode);
604   if (expect_cipher && server_params.selected_cipher != CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305) {
605     log_error("Server selected unsupported cipher algorithm: %u", server_params.selected_cipher);
606     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
607     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
608   }
609   if (!expect_cipher && server_params.selected_cipher != CIPHER_ALGO_NONE) {
610     log_error("Server chose cipher %u but client requested no encryption", server_params.selected_cipher);
611     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
612     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
613   }
614 
615   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Protocol negotiation completed successfully");
616 
617   // Step 0.5: Send CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO with expected server key (multi-key support)
618   // This allows the server to select the correct identity key from g_server_identity_keys[]
619   if (g_crypto_ctx.verify_server_key && strlen(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key) > 0) {
620     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Sending expected server key to support multi-key selection");
621 
622     // Parse expected server key to get the Ed25519 public key
623     public_key_t expected_keys[MAX_CLIENTS];
624     size_t num_expected_keys = 0;
625 
626     if (parse_public_keys(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key, expected_keys, &num_expected_keys, MAX_CLIENTS) == 0 &&
627         num_expected_keys > 0) {
628       // Send the first expected key (client should only have one expected server key)
629       // Format: 32-byte Ed25519 public key
630       log_debug("Sending CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO with expected server key for multi-key selection");
631       result = send_packet(socket, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO, expected_keys[0].key, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
632 
633       if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
634         FATAL(result, "Failed to send CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO packet");
635       }
636 
637       // Log the key fingerprint for debugging
638       char hex_key[65];
639       pubkey_to_hex(expected_keys[0].key, hex_key);
640       log_debug("Sent expected server key: %s", hex_key);
641     } else {
642       log_warn("Failed to parse expected server key '%s' - server will use default key",
643                g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key);
644     }
645   }
646 
647   // Step 1: Receive server's public key and send our public key
648   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Starting key exchange");
649   result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange_socket(&g_crypto_ctx, socket);
650   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
651 #ifdef _WIN32
652     // On Windows: Cleanup capture resources before exiting to prevent Media Foundation threads from hanging exit()
653     // Media Foundation creates background COM threads that can block exit() if not properly shut down
654     capture_cleanup();
655 #endif
656     FATAL(result, "Crypto key exchange failed");
657   }
658   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Key exchange completed successfully");
659 
660   // SECURITY: Warn when server requires client verification but client has no identity key
661   bool client_has_identity = (g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519);
662 
663   if (g_crypto_ctx.require_client_auth && !client_has_identity) {
664     // Server requires client verification but client has no identity key
665     log_warn("Server requires client verification but client has no identity key");
666 
667     // Check if we're running interactively (stdin is a terminal)
668     // In debug builds with CLAUDECODE, skip interactive prompts (LLM can't type)
669 #ifndef NDEBUG
670     bool skip_interactive = platform_getenv("CLAUDECODE") != NULL;
671 #else
672     bool skip_interactive = false;
673 #endif
674     if (!skip_interactive && platform_is_interactive()) {
675       // Interactive mode - prompt user for confirmation
676       // Lock terminal for the warning message
677       bool previous_terminal_state = log_lock_terminal();
678 
679       log_plain("\n"
680                 "@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@\n"
681                 "@  WARNING: CLIENT AUTHENTICATION REQUIRED                                    @\n"
682                 "@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@\n"
683                 "\n"
684                 "The server requires client authentication (--client-keys enabled),\n"
685                 "but you have not provided a client identity key with --key.\n"
686                 "\n"
687                 "To connect to this server, you need to:\n"
688                 "  1. Generate an Ed25519 key: ssh-keygen -t ed25519\n"
689                 "  2. Add the public key to the server's --client-keys list\n"
690                 "  3. Connect with: ascii-chat client --key /path/to/private/key\n");
691 
692       // Unlock before prompt (prompt_yes_no handles its own terminal locking)
693       log_unlock_terminal(previous_terminal_state);
694 
695       // Prompt user - default is No since this will likely fail
696       if (!platform_prompt_yes_no("Do you want to continue anyway (this will likely fail)", false)) {
697         log_plain("Connection aborted by user.");
698         exit(0); // User declined - exit cleanly
699       }
700 
701       log_plain("Warning: Continuing without client identity key (connection may fail).\n");
702     } else {
703       // Non-interactive mode (background/script) - just log warning and continue
704       log_warn("Non-interactive mode: Continuing without client identity key (connection may fail)");
705     }
706   }
707 
708   // Step 2: Receive auth challenge and send response
709   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO: Sending auth response to server...");
710   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Starting auth response");
711   result = crypto_handshake_client_auth_response_socket(&g_crypto_ctx, socket);
712   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
713     FATAL(result, "Crypto authentication failed");
714   }
715   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO: Auth response sent successfully");
716   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Auth response completed successfully");
717 
718   // Check if handshake completed during auth response (no authentication needed)
719   if (g_crypto_ctx.state == CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
720     // Propagate encryption flag to crypto context
721     g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx.encrypt_data = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode);
722     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(debug, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "client_crypto_handshake",
723                        "Crypto handshake completed successfully (no authentication)");
724     return 0;
725   }
726 
727   // Step 3: Receive handshake complete message
728   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Waiting for handshake complete message");
729   result = crypto_handshake_client_complete_socket(&g_crypto_ctx, socket);
730   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
731     FATAL(result, "Crypto handshake completion failed");
732   }
733 
734   // Propagate encryption flag to crypto context
735   g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx.encrypt_data = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode);
736 
737   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(debug, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "client_crypto_handshake", "Crypto handshake completed successfully");
738   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Handshake completed successfully, state=%d", g_crypto_ctx.state);
739   return 0;
740 }




References buffer_pool_free(), capture_cleanup(),
    CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED,
    crypto_handshake_client_auth_response_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_client_complete_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_set_parameters(), g_crypto_ctx,
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), log_lock_terminal(),
    log_unlock_terminal(), parse_public_keys(),
    platform_getenv(), platform_prompt_yes_no(),
    pubkey_to_hex(), receive_packet(),
    send_crypto_capabilities_packet(), send_packet(), and
    send_protocol_version_packet().


Referenced by client_main(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), and
  server_connection_establish().






int
  client_crypto_init (void )


Initialize client crypto handshake. Initialize client crypto
    handshake




Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 208 of file
  src/client/crypto.c.


208                              {
209   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Starting crypto initialization");
210 
211   // Reset if already initialized (allows reinit on reconnection)
212   if (lifecycle_reset(&g_crypto_lc)) {
213     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Already initialized, resetting for reinit");
214     crypto_handshake_destroy(&g_crypto_ctx);
215   }
216 
217   // Check if encryption is disabled
218   if (GET_OPTION(no_encrypt)) {
219     log_info("Encryption disabled");
220     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Encryption disabled, returning 0");
221     return 0;
222   }
223 
224   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Initializing crypto handshake context");
225 
226   // Check if we have an SSH key, password, or neither
227   int result;
228   bool is_ssh_key = false;
229   private_key_t private_key;
230 
231   // Get string options safely
232   const options_t *opts = options_get();
233   const char *encrypt_key = opts && opts->encrypt_key[0] != '\0' ? opts->encrypt_key : "";
234   const char *password = opts && opts->password[0] != '\0' ? opts->password : "";
235   const char *address = opts && opts->address[0] != '\0' ? opts->address : "localhost";
236   int port = opts ? opts->port : OPT_PORT_INT_DEFAULT;
237   const char *server_key = opts && opts->server_key[0] != '\0' ? opts->server_key : "";
238 
239   // Load client private key if provided via --key
240   if (strlen(encrypt_key) > 0) {
241     // --key supports file-based authentication (SSH keys, GPG keys via gpg:keyid)
242 
243     // For SSH key files (not gpg:keyid format), validate the file exists
244     if (strncmp(encrypt_key, "gpg:", 4) != 0) {
245       if (validate_ssh_key_file(encrypt_key) != 0) {
246         return -1;
247       }
248     }
249 
250     // Parse key (handles SSH files and gpg:keyid format)
251     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Loading private key for authentication: %s", encrypt_key);
252     if (parse_private_key(encrypt_key, &private_key) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
253       log_debug("Successfully parsed SSH private key");
254       log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Parsed key type=%d, KEY_TYPE_ED25519=%d", private_key.type, KEY_TYPE_ED25519);
255       is_ssh_key = true;
256     } else {
257       log_error("Failed to parse SSH key file: %s", encrypt_key);
258       log_error("This may be due to:");
259       log_error("  - Wrong password for encrypted key");
260       log_error("  - Unsupported key type (only Ed25519 is currently supported)");
261       log_error("  - Corrupted key file");
262       log_error("");
263       log_error("Note: RSA and ECDSA keys are not yet supported");
264       log_error("To generate an Ed25519 key: ssh-keygen -t ed25519");
265       return -1;
266     }
267   }
268 
269   if (is_ssh_key) {
270     // Use SSH private key for authentication
271     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Using SSH key for authentication");
272 
273     // Initialize crypto context (generates ephemeral X25519 keys)
274     result = crypto_handshake_init("crypto_client_local", &g_crypto_ctx, false); // false = client
275     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
276       FATAL(result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
277     }
278 
279     // Store the Ed25519 keys for authentication
280     memcpy(&g_crypto_ctx.client_private_key, &private_key, sizeof(private_key_t));
281 
282     // Extract Ed25519 public key from private key
283     g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
284     memcpy(g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.key, private_key.public_key, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
285     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.comment, private_key.key_comment,
286                  sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.comment) - 1);
287 
288     // Extract GPG key ID if this is a GPG key (format: "gpg:KEYID")
289     if (strncmp(encrypt_key, "gpg:", 4) == 0) {
290       const char *key_id = encrypt_key + 4;
291       size_t key_id_len = strlen(key_id);
292       // Accept 8, 16, or 40 character GPG key IDs (short/long/full fingerprint)
293       if (key_id_len == 8 || key_id_len == 16 || key_id_len == 40) {
294         SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id, key_id, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id));
295         log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Extracted client GPG key ID (%zu chars): %s", key_id_len,
296                   g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id);
297       } else {
298         log_warn("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Invalid GPG key ID length: %zu (expected 8, 16, or 40)", key_id_len);
299         g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id[0] = '\0';
300       }
301     } else {
302       // Not a GPG key, clear the field
303       g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id[0] = '\0';
304     }
305 
306     // SSH key is already configured in the handshake context above
307     // No additional setup needed - SSH keys are used only for authentication
308 
309     // Clear the temporary private_key variable (we've already copied it to g_crypto_ctx)
310     sodium_memzero(&private_key, sizeof(private_key));
311 
312     // If password is also provided, derive password key for dual authentication
313     if (strlen(password) > 0) {
314       log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Password also provided, deriving password key");
315       crypto_result_t crypto_result = crypto_derive_password_key(&g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx, password);
316       if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
317         log_error("Failed to derive password key: %s", crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
318         return -1;
319       }
320       g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx.has_password = true;
321       log_debug("Password authentication enabled alongside SSH key");
322     }
323 
324   } else if (strlen(GET_OPTION(password)) > 0) {
325     // Password provided - use password-based initialization
326     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Using password authentication");
327     result = crypto_handshake_init_with_password("crypto_client_local", &g_crypto_ctx, false,
328                                                  GET_OPTION(password)); // false = client
329     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
330       FATAL(result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake with password");
331     }
332   } else {
333     // No password or SSH key - use standard initialization with random keys
334     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Using standard initialization");
335     result = crypto_handshake_init("crypto_client_local", &g_crypto_ctx, false); // false = client
336     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
337       FATAL(result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
338     }
339   }
340 
341   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: crypto_handshake_init succeeded");
342 
343   // Set up server connection info for known_hosts
344   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.server_hostname, address, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.server_hostname) - 1);
345   const char *server_ip = server_connection_get_ip();
346   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: server_connection_get_ip() returned: '%s'", server_ip ? server_ip : "NULL");
347   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.server_ip, server_ip ? server_ip : "", sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.server_ip) - 1);
348 
349   // Use port from server_connection_set_port() if available (e.g., from WebSocket URL),
350   // otherwise use the port from options (TCP/default path)
351   int connection_port = server_connection_get_port();
352   g_crypto_ctx.server_port = (uint16_t)(connection_port > 0 ? connection_port : port);
353   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Set server_ip='%s', server_port=%u", g_crypto_ctx.server_ip, g_crypto_ctx.server_port);
354 
355   // Configure server key verification if specified
356   if (strlen(server_key) > 0) {
357     g_crypto_ctx.verify_server_key = true;
358     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key, server_key, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key) - 1);
359     log_debug("Server key verification enabled: %s", server_key);
360   }
361 
362   // If --require-client-verify is set, perform ACDS session lookup for server identity
363   if (GET_OPTION(require_client_verify) && strlen(GET_OPTION(session_string)) > 0) {
364     log_debug("--require-client-verify enabled: performing ACDS session lookup for '%s'", GET_OPTION(session_string));
365 
366     // Connect to ACDS server (configurable via --acds-server and --acds-port options)
367     acds_client_config_t acds_config;
368     acds_client_config_init_defaults(&acds_config);
369     SAFE_STRNCPY(acds_config.server_address, GET_OPTION(discovery_server), sizeof(acds_config.server_address));
370     acds_config.server_port = GET_OPTION(discovery_port);
371     acds_config.timeout_ms = 5 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;
372 
373     // ACDS key verification (optional in debug builds, only if --discovery-service-key is provided)
374     if (strlen(GET_OPTION(discovery_service_key)) > 0) {
375       log_debug("Verifying ACDS server key for %s...", acds_config.server_address);
376       uint8_t acds_pubkey[32];
377       asciichat_error_t verify_result =
378           discovery_keys_verify(acds_config.server_address, GET_OPTION(discovery_service_key), acds_pubkey);
379       if (verify_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
380         log_error("ACDS key verification failed for %s", acds_config.server_address);
381         return -1;
382       }
383       log_debug("ACDS server key verified successfully");
384     }
385 #ifndef NDEBUG
386     else {
387       log_debug("Skipping ACDS key verification (debug build, no --discovery-service-key provided)");
388     }
389 #endif
390 
391     acds_client_t acds_client;
392     asciichat_error_t acds_result = acds_client_connect(&acds_client, &acds_config);
393     if (acds_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
394       log_error("Failed to connect to ACDS server at %s:%d", acds_config.server_address, acds_config.server_port);
395       return -1;
396     }
397 
398     // Perform SESSION_LOOKUP to get server's identity
399     acds_session_lookup_result_t lookup_result;
400     acds_result = acds_session_lookup(&acds_client, GET_OPTION(session_string), &lookup_result);
401     acds_client_disconnect(&acds_client);
402 
403     if (acds_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || !lookup_result.found) {
404       log_error("ACDS session lookup failed for '%s': %s", GET_OPTION(session_string),
405                 lookup_result.found ? "session not found" : "lookup error");
406       return -1;
407     }
408 
409     // Convert server's Ed25519 public key (32 bytes) to hex string
410     char server_key_hex[65]; // 32 bytes * 2 + null terminator
411     for (size_t i = 0; i < 32; i++) {
412       SAFE_SNPRINTF(&server_key_hex[i * 2], 3, "%02x", lookup_result.host_pubkey[i]);
413     }
414     server_key_hex[64] = '\0';
415 
416     // Set expected server key for verification during handshake
417     g_crypto_ctx.verify_server_key = true;
418     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key, server_key_hex, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key) - 1);
419     log_debug("ACDS session lookup succeeded - server identity will be verified");
420     log_debug("Expected server key (from ACDS): %s", server_key_hex);
421   }
422 
423   // Mark initialization as complete
424   lifecycle_init(&g_crypto_lc, "client_crypto");
425 
426   log_debug("Client crypto handshake initialized");
427   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Initialization complete");
428   return 0;
429 }






References acds_client_config_init_defaults(),
    acds_client_connect(), acds_client_disconnect(),
    acds_session_lookup(), crypto_derive_password_key(),
    crypto_handshake_destroy(), crypto_handshake_init(),
    crypto_handshake_init_with_password(),
    crypto_result_to_string(), discovery_keys_verify(),
    g_crypto_ctx, lifecycle_init(), lifecycle_reset(),
    options_get(), parse_private_key(),
    server_connection_get_ip(), server_connection_get_port(), and
    validate_ssh_key_file().


Referenced by client_main(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), connection_attempt_websocket(), and
    server_connection_establish().

  crypto_client_cleanup (void )


Cleanup crypto client resources. Cleanup crypto client
  resources

  src/client/crypto.c.


813                                  {
814   // Shutdown if initialized
815   if (lifecycle_shutdown(&g_crypto_lc)) {
816     crypto_handshake_destroy(&g_crypto_ctx);
817     log_debug("Client crypto handshake cleaned up");
818   }
819 }

    and lifecycle_shutdown().

  crypto_client_decrypt_packet (const uint8_t * ciphertext, size_t
  ciphertext_len, uint8_t * plaintext, size_t plaintext_size, size_t *
  plaintext_len)


Decrypt a received packet. Decrypt a received packet




Parameters


ciphertext Encrypted data to decrypt


ciphertext_len Length of encrypted data


plaintext Output buffer for decrypted data


plaintext_size Size of output buffer


plaintext_len Output length of decrypted data


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure

int
  crypto_client_initiate_rekey (void )


Initiate session rekeying (client-initiated). Initiate session
    rekeying (client-initiated)




Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 846 of file
  src/client/crypto.c.


846                                        {
847   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_crypto_lc) || !crypto_client_is_ready()) {
848     log_error("Cannot initiate rekey: crypto not initialized or not ready");
849     return -1;
850   }
851 
852   socket_t socket = server_connection_get_socket();
853   if (socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
854     log_error("Cannot initiate rekey: invalid socket");
855     return -1;
856   }
857 
858   asciichat_error_t result = crypto_handshake_rekey_request(&g_crypto_ctx, socket);
859   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
860     log_error("Failed to send REKEY_REQUEST: %d", result);
861     return -1;
862   }
863 
864   return 0;
865 }

885     return -1;

  crypto_client_send_rekey_complete (void )
